rulings.Case Title: LINDA CATRON VS. COUNTRYWIDE BANK, FSB ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: To Reconsider Defendants Motion To Dismiss 11/15/2023
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF LINDA CATRON To Reconsider Defendants Motion To Dismiss 11/15/2023 is DENIED. Defendant's motion to dismiss (and joinder) were first set for hearing for July 21, 2023. Per Plaintiff's request the Court continued the hearing several times. One day before one of the continued hearing dates, on September 14, 2023, Plaintiff filed a request for continuance and a purported response/opposition to the motion. That response was untimely and was substantively deficient and devoid of any evidence and authority. The Court gave Plaintiff another opportunity to file an opposition on the merits addressing issues raised in the Motion to Dismiss by November 1, 2023. Plaintiff did not avail herself of this opportunity. Having considered the moving papers and the filings by the P submitted after the original filing of the Motion to Dismiss and before the hearing on November 15, 2023, the Court granted the Motion to Dismiss (and joinder). Plaintiff's Motion for Reconsideration filed on November 27, 2023 does not meet the requirements of CCP 1008. The motion mentions CCP 473, but the requirements of that code section are also not met. The Court notes that it did not receive any proposed orders for execution after November 15, 2023 hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA RHODES VS. TY INVESTMENT, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT TY INVESTMENT, LLC DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the causes of action 7-9 is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition to the demurrer to these causes of action and it appears from the opposition papers on file that Plaintiff believes that there are only 6 causes of action asserted in the operative complaint. (Opposition 1:6-9). Demurrer to causes of action 1-3, 5 and 6 is OVERRULED. Complaint alleges facts regarding applicability of HBOR. Challenging the veracity of these allegations is not a proper function of the demurrer. The Court will not consider documents attached to the moving papers on the demurrer. Demurrer to the 4th causes of action for negligence is sustained with leave to amend to be re-asserted only if Plaintiff may do so in good faith in light of Sheen v. Wells Fargo (2022) 12 Cal.5th 905. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
